description: Professional open-source astrology software currently in development.
cookieModalNoCookies: This site does not use any cookies.
cookieModalFormInfo: Data submitted via the Contact us form are sent directly to our email inboxes.
about1Text: Adjusts the screen display for better readability and eye comfort. Light mode is ideal for use in daylight, while dark mode reduces glare and is gentler on the eyes in low light or at night.
about2Text: With more than 20,000 horoscopes of Czech and international personalities, you have instant access to a rich source of information. KEFER includes charts of important figures from politics, art, science, and sport, making deeper study of astrology easier.
about3Text: Creating your own labels improves organisation and work with horoscopes. You can mark personal charts, famous personalities, or events, and use multiple categories, custom colours, names, and structures.
fCarousel1: New natal, horary, and mundane chart
fCarousel2: Load personal charts and the personality database
astrolabText: Allows users to set and edit place, time, and time zone for each chart.
keferText: Jan Kefer (31 January 1906 – 3 December 1941) was a prominent Czech hermeticist active between the two world wars. He chaired the Universalia society, which brought together Czech hermeticists. With respect for Jan Kefer and with the permission of his son Reginald, we named the software KEFER.
openSourceText:
We chose the open-source path because we believe it allows a broad community of developers and astrologers to help shape KEFER with their ideas and improvements. The entire source code is openly shared on {github}, and we are working to document it clearly so individual components can be modified more easily.
For development, we use {rust} (specifically {tauri}), which can integrate with powerful {python} libraries while also enabling precise interface work in {sveltekit}.
donateText: We value every form of support that helps us continue developing and maintaining the program. You can support us financially, share ideas and feedback, or join our team if you are a designer or developer.
downloadText: KEFER is currently in development. You can follow the current progress through regular updates or on our Facebook profile.
contactText: We are a small team building an open-source astrology application with long-term community collaboration in mind.
visionText: We are developing Kefer in several language versions with the goal of creating a modern application with an intuitive user interface. The current features are only the beginning — we will gradually add new ones and improve the existing ones. Thanks to the open-source code, both users and developers can actively contribute and support the further development of Kefer.
homeBlogTeaser: News, updates, and articles about the project's development.
homeAboutUsTeaser: Our vision, ways to support the project, and how to contact us.
aboutAppMeta: Overview of Kefer Astrology features and capabilities.
aboutUsMeta: We are a team of enthusiasts building open-source astrology software together.
aboutUsLead: We are four enthusiasts who decided to build open-source astrology software together.
newsMetaDescription: News and updates from the Kefer Astrology blog.
contactDescription: Have an idea to improve Kefer? Want to work with us? Contact us.
